title: Language Teaching Application and Research Center - Why Turkish?
content:
Language Teaching Application and Research Center - Why Turkish?

Turkish, also known as the Turkish language or Türkiye Turkish, is one of the Altaic languages spoken from the Balkans in the west to the shores of the Caspian Sea in the east. Belonging to the Altaic branch of the Ural-Altaic language family, Turkish is one of the richest languages in the world and ranks as the 5th most spoken language globally.

Turkish is spoken primarily in Turkey and Cyprus, as well as in Balkan countries, the Caucasus, Iran, and Central Asian countries. Spanning a wide geographical area from Europe to Asia, Turkish is steadily advancing as a strategic and important language. Learning Turkish provides business and educational opportunities not only in Turkey, but also across the Middle East, Europe, Asia, and Africa.

This content was updated on 12/08/2025.
